title: Direct behavior review parses and runtime-decodes exact test literals
context: Direct review now parses real test calls and sees modified existing tests, but exact replay still used source-spelled escape sequences rather than the runtime node:test name. A literal such as escaped \u0028exact\u0029 registered as escaped (exact) but produced a non-executable candidate name.
decision: Direct behavior review derives candidates only from parsed literal node:test test()/it() calls, decodes JavaScript string escapes to the registered runtime name, and recognizes an existing case as modified only when the fixing diff adds a line inside that exact call. Exact patterns are built from the decoded name; all receipts remain content addressed and non-authoritative until separate activation.
consequences: Escaped literal test names execute as exact selected events rather than false zero-test passes.
Assertions added to existing named tests enter the same fail-to-pass review loop.
Quoted test-like fixture text remains inert and legacy real-test candidate identities remain stable.
alternatives_rejected: Match the raw source spelling: rejected because node:test registers the decoded value and exact replay would execute zero selected tests.
Use a broad regex pattern: rejected because punctuation and escapes can match the wrong test or no test.
Fall back to helper-symbol structure: rejected because implementation structure does not prove durable behavior.
